spec_match.rationale: The page now names and exercises the current Web Printing API draft surfaces: window.printing.getPrinters(), WebPrinter.cachedAttributes(), WebPrinter.fetchAttributes(), WebPrinter.submitPrintJob(), WebPrintJob.attributes(), cancel(), and onjobstatechange.

interactive.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp exercised receipt edits, add/remove item, successful submit, unsupported-ticket DataError, malformed-document DataError, and cancellation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and concept lede now describe the receipt job lab's actual WebPrintingManager flow and receipt-specific failure handling.

edge_cases.rationale: The lab now covers ordinary-page/IWA unavailability, unsupported media source, malformed PDF data, and job cancellation with job-state reasons.

references.rationale: The see also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5100352332627968, the Web Printing draft, RFC 8011, and the feature conformance probe.

design_system.rationale: The page uses design-system tokens, pairs button backgrounds with colors, has no raw color literals, and DevTools reported no accessibility or console issues after the form-field name fix.

summary: Open questions resolved. Before the fix, chrome-devtools-mcp confirmed the page lacked a ChromeStatus link and only exposed navigator.printing.getPrinters() plus an unavailable-API alert; it did not surface fetchAttributes(), submitPrintJob(), WebPrintJob attributes, cancellation, DataError, or IWA boundary details. After the fix, DevTools verified editor updates for store name, tax, paper width, item rename, add item, and remove item; the receipt job lab logged getPrinters(), cachedAttributes(), fetchAttributes(), submitPrintJob(), WebPrintJob.attributes(), and onjobstatechange through completed; unsupported media source and malformed PDF each surfaced DataError; cancel() moved the job to aborted with job-canceled-by-user. The feature conformance route reported 8 pass / 0 fail in Chrome 148, with clean console and successful network requests.
